content: Statins are HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors that block cholesterol synthesis rate-limiting step in liver. Mechanism: competitive inhibition of HMG-CoA reductase reduces intracellular cholesterol, upregulating LDL receptors, increasing hepatic LDL clearance, lowering circulating LDL-C by 30-60% depending on potency and dose. Pleiotropic effects: improve endothelial function, stabilize atherosclerotic plaques, reduce inflammation (lower hs-CRP), decrease thrombosis risk. Statin intensity: high-intensity (atorvastatin 40-80mg, rosuvastatin 20-40mg) lowers LDL ≥50%; moderate-intensity (atorvastatin 10-20mg, rosuvastatin 5-10mg, simvastatin 20-40mg, pravastatin 40-80mg) lowers 30-49%; low-intensity lowers <30%. Rosuvastatin and atorvastatin most potent (longest half-lives). Lipophilic statins (simvastatin, lovastatin, atorvastatin) may have more muscle penetration; hydrophilic (pravastatin, rosuvastatin) potentially fewer drug interactions.
